payload.intent: Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

payload.content: PRD for State.read_events_since cursor tail read plus (run_id, event_id) index. Verified every factual claim against supervisor/state.py: current events index is (run_id, ts), recent_events is a reversed last-N read, read_dual_agent_gate_events orders ASC, write_event is append-only, run_watches.last_event_id exists, and no read_events_since exists. Gap is correctly identified; contracts complete with forbidden outcomes; grill findings resolved/waived; decomposed into 4 testable slices. Accept.

assumptions_to_verify: SQLite chooses idx_events_run_event for run_id=? AND event_id>? ORDER BY event_id ASC at implementation time
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS in SCHEMA bootstrap reliably backfills the index on previously-opened old databases
contradictions_checked: Checked recent_events vs read_events_since shape divergence — no P5 contradiction since new method, recent_events untouched
Checked whether (run_id, event_id) index is redundant given rowid — it is not; it avoids sort for run-scoped range scan
Checked that no read_events_since already exists — confirmed absent
missing_evidence: No RED tests or implementation exist yet to confirm the tail query is actually index-served
No EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output captured for the proposed query against a real db
critical_review.strongest_objection: P2 binds a user-visible promise to EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN naming idx_events_run_event; because event_id is the INTEGER PRIMARY KEY (rowid), SQLite may serve ordering via the PK and the plan text is version-sensitive, so the assertion could be brittle or falsely reassuring.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the (run_id, event_id) index does not actually change the query plan (i.e., PK/rowid already serves it) would make P2's index a no-op and warrant revise; or discovery that recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events share code paths the new method would alter would warrant revise.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content: TDD plan for State.read_events_since cursor tail plus (run_id, event_id) index reviewed against supervisor/state.py. All gate factual claims verified: recent_events is last-N, no read_events_since exists, index is (run_id, ts), and the only existing tail consumer (telegram_progress.py:43) filters in Python not via WHERE event_id > ?. Plan hits the true public boundary, covers forbidden outcomes, non-contiguous fixture, EXPLAIN, old-DB bootstrap, and reconnect exact-once. ACCEPT with three non-blocking revisions.

assumptions_to_verify: The reconnect test will use an offline batch larger than limit to exercise the paging loop
read_events_since returns nested payload plus event_id/ts/source/kind so the consumer can advance the cursor
Adding idx_events_run_event does not require dropping idx_events_run (plan keeps both)
contradictions_checked: Gate claim 'no generic after-this-id read' vs existence of the watch-notification consumer: no contradiction; telegram_progress.py filters in Python, not SQL
P5 'existing reads unchanged' vs adding a composite index that could alter recent_events query plan: no behavioral contradiction; results and shape are unchanged
Non-contiguous tolerance vs AUTOINCREMENT: consistent; AUTOINCREMENT never reuses ids so deletes create real gaps
missing_evidence: Explicit assertion that catch-up loops across multiple limit-bounded pages
A test mapping PRD P1 non-positive-limit -> [] outcome
Confirmation that EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN reliably selects idx_events_run_event on a small unanalyzed test DB rather than a full scan
critical_review.strongest_objection: The reconnect proof (test_event_tail_consumer_catches_up_after_disconnect_once) does not specify that the offline event batch exceeds limit, so it may only prove single-page catch-up while the actual resumability requirement is a paging loop that drains the tail until empty; a consumer that stops after one limited page would silently drop events and the test could still pass.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the reconnect test asserts only a single read or omits the empty-tail terminating poll, or that the new read modifies recent_events / read_dual_agent_gate_events / telegram_progress behavior, would move this from accept-with-revisions toward revise-before-advance.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content: TDD plan reviewed against live source. All current-behavior claims verified: recent_events is DESC+reverse last-N (state.py:428), idx_events_run is (run_id,ts) (state.py:61), read_dual_agent_gate_events is ASC (state.py:441), events.event_id is AUTOINCREMENT (state.py:54), no read_events_since exists. Plan uses correct State boundary, maps tests to issues/PRD promises, has genuine RED, and includes the exactly-once disconnect/reconnect proof, non-contiguous-id fixture, EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN, old-DB bootstrap, and existing-reads regression guard. Two low-severity coverage gaps (non-positive limit, payload-shape assertion) are revise-during-GREEN notes, not blockers. Accept.

assumptions_to_verify: GREEN keeps idx_events_run while adding idx_events_run_event (additive, not replacing)
read_events_since returns nested payload, not flattened like recent_events
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS in SCHEMA bootstrap applies idempotently to already-open old databases
contradictions_checked: Plan says current index is (run_id, ts) — confirmed state.py:61
Plan says recent_events is last-N then reversed — confirmed state.py:428
Plan says no read_events_since exists — confirmed via grep
Plan claims AUTOINCREMENT permits gaps — confirmed state.py:54
missing_evidence: No executed test run (plan stage); test_status unknown
No explicit assertion that read_events_since returns source/kind/ts alongside payload and event_id
critical_review.strongest_objection: Two PRD implementation decisions are unpinned by named tests: non-positive limit returns [] (prd.md:108) and the read_events_since nested payload shape distinct from recent_events flattening (prd.md:106). A consumer could regress these without a failing test.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If the disconnect/reconnect test did not assert exact-once delivery plus an empty subsequent poll, or if adding the new index were specified to drop/replace idx_events_run, I would move to revise/deny.

payload.intent: Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: The plan's described gap (last-N recent_events, no event_id>? cursor read, (run_id,ts) index, no generic after-id read) matches live source
The plan touches only supervisor/state.py and tests/test_state_event_ledger.py and preserves recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior
Non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT ids are tolerated by range cursor semantics with no arithmetic paging

criteria: All plan claims about current schema/index/methods match live source: met
read_events_since confirmed net-new, not pre-existing: met
Old-DB index upgrade mechanism (SCHEMA re-run) confirmed: met
Query-plan test robustness against rowid-alias planning: partially met (depends on implementer following R1 / using multi-run fixture)
Full suite green: unverified (not executed at gate)
evidence: planned verification: uv run --extra dev pytest -q
accept: advance the implementation_plan gate; the plan accurately maps to live source and is surgical and traceable

revise-note (non-blocking): align the index query-plan test with implementation-plan R1 (assert no SCAN events + indexed SEARCH) rather than issues.md ISS-2's stricter literal requirement that idx_events_run_event be named, since event_id is an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY rowid alias and SQLite may serve the tail via the rowid range on small/single-run fixtures
keep idx_events_run (run_id, ts) intact and add idx_events_run_event (run_id, event_id) additively via CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS
new read_events_since must nest payload (not flatten like recent_events) and guard limit<=0 -> []
confidence.rationale: Every structural claim in the plan was verified line-for-line against supervisor/state.py, the target test file exists, and the plan is surgical and traceable. Confidence is held below 0.9 because nothing was executed at the gate and one genuine test-design risk (rowid-alias query plan vs ISS-2 strict assertion) is only fully resolved at implementation time.

payload.content: Implementation plan for the S1 event-tail cursor read plus idx_events_run_event index verified against live supervisor/state.py. All structural claims (current (run_id,ts) index, AUTOINCREMENT event_id rowid, recent_events flattened shape, read_dual_agent_gate_events ASC ordering, SCHEMA re-run on open, absence of read_events_since) confirmed accurate. Plan is surgical (state.py + test file), traceable P1-P5, and respects non-goals. Accept/advance with one non-blocking note: the query-plan test must use the plan's softened R1 invariant (and ideally a multi-run fixture) because event_id is a rowid alias and SQLite may serve the tail via the integer primary key rather than the new composite index.

assumptions_to_verify: The query-plan test will assert the R1 invariant (no SCAN events + indexed SEARCH) rather than hard-requiring the index name, and/or uses a multi-run fixture
Deleting an intermediate committed event in the non-contiguous fixture does not violate any FK/trigger and leaves a real id gap
Opening an old-style DB (events + idx_events_run only) through State() actually creates idx_events_run_event via the existing executescript(SCHEMA) path
contradictions_checked: issues.md ISS-2 strict 'uses idx_events_run_event' vs implementation-plan R1 softened 'no SCAN / prefer detecting index' -> real divergence; plan's softer invariant is the correct call

Intent mentions run_watches.last_event_id lacks a generic after-id read vs plan not modifying run_watches -> consistent: plan adds the generic primitive (read_events_since) without rewiring watches, matching the stated scope
recent_events flattened 'id' shape vs new 'event_id'+nested 'payload' shape -> intentional and non-conflicting
missing_evidence: Actual EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output for the tail query on both a single-run and a multi-run fixture
Full dev test suite run result (uv run --extra dev pytest -q)
Confirmation the new method's payload-nested shape does not break any existing caller expectations
critical_review.strongest_objection: Because event_id is an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT (a rowid alias), SQLite may satisfy 'WHERE run_id=? AND event_id>? ORDER BY event_id ASC' via the integer-primary-key range rather than idx_events_run_event, particularly on a small single-run test fixture. issues.md ISS-2 literally requires the plan to show idx_events_run_event in EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN, which could make that test flaky even though the index is correct and valuable for multi-run ledgers.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that read_events_since already exists with different semantics, that idx_events_run is load-bearing in a way the new index would regress, or that an existing caller depends on the new method name with a flattened shape — any of these would move me from accept to revise/deny.

assumptions_to_verify: The query-plan test will assert the R1 invariant (no SCAN events + indexed SEARCH) rather than hard-requiring the index name, and/or uses a multi-run fixture
Deleting an intermediate committed event in the non-contiguous fixture does not violate any FK/trigger and leaves a real id gap
Opening an old-style DB (events + idx_events_run only) through State() actually creates idx_events_run_event via the existing executescript(SCHEMA) path
contradictions_checked: issues.md ISS-2 strict 'uses idx_events_run_event' vs implementation-plan R1 softened 'no SCAN / prefer detecting index' -> real divergence; plan's softer invariant is the correct call

Intent mentions run_watches.last_event_id lacks a generic after-id read vs plan not modifying run_watches -> consistent: plan adds the generic primitive (read_events_since) without rewiring watches, matching the stated scope
recent_events flattened 'id' shape vs new 'event_id'+nested 'payload' shape -> intentional and non-conflicting
missing_evidence: Actual EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output for the tail query on both a single-run and a multi-run fixture
Full dev test suite run result (uv run --extra dev pytest -q)
Confirmation the new method's payload-nested shape does not break any existing caller expectations
critical_review.strongest_objection: Because event_id is an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT (a rowid alias), SQLite may satisfy 'WHERE run_id=? AND event_id>? ORDER BY event_id ASC' via the integer-primary-key range rather than idx_events_run_event, particularly on a small single-run test fixture. issues.md ISS-2 literally requires the plan to show idx_events_run_event in EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN, which could make that test flaky even though the index is correct and valuable for multi-run ledgers.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that read_events_since already exists with different semantics, that idx_events_run is load-bearing in a way the new index would regress, or that an existing caller depends on the new method name with a flattened shape — any of these would move me from accept to revise/deny.

decisions: accept: advance the implementation_plan gate; the plan accurately maps to live source and is surgical and traceable

revise-note (non-blocking): align the index query-plan test with implementation-plan R1 (assert no SCAN events + indexed SEARCH) rather than issues.md ISS-2's stricter literal requirement that idx_events_run_event be named, since event_id is an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY rowid alias and SQLite may serve the tail via the rowid range on small/single-run fixtures
keep idx_events_run (run_id, ts) intact and add idx_events_run_event (run_id, event_id) additively via CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS
new read_events_since must nest payload (not flatten like recent_events) and guard limit<=0 -> []
objections: issues.md ISS-2 acceptance ('EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN shows an index search using idx_events_run_event') is stricter than what SQLite reliably delivers when event_id is a rowid alias; on a single-run fixture the planner may use the integer-primary-key range (a SEARCH, not a SCAN). Mitigation already in plan R1; recommend a multi-run fixture to make the composite index the genuinely cheaper plan.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

row-count/fixture size that makes the index search genuinely preferred over a rowid scan
full-suite green result (not expected until execution gate)

ORDER BY event_id ASC via the rowid b-tree or a full table scan on small test tables, so the assertion could either flake or pass without actually exercising idx_events_run_event.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that the P2 query-plan assertion is trivially satisfiable (passes even when the new index is absent) or that adding idx_events_run_event measurably regresses write_event throughput would push this from accept to revise.

payload.content: Independent verification confirms Claude's source-gap claims and surgical two-file plan. SCHEMA-on-open index bootstrap, P1-P5 traceability, and non-goals are coherent. Accept implementation_plan gate; execution must tighten P2 query-plan proof because R1 no-SCAN alone is satisfiable via idx_events_run without idx_events_run_event.

assumptions_to_verify: Execution asserts idx_events_run_event in EXPLAIN (not just no-SCAN) or uses differential fixture
Non-contiguous DELETE fixture leaves monotonic AUTOINCREMENT for subsequent inserts
after_event_id None coerced to 0 matches explicit 0 semantics
contradictions_checked: run_watches.last_event_id gap vs plan not wiring watches: scoped non-goal, adds primitive only
issues.md ISS-2 strict index-name assertion vs plan R1 softened invariant: real tension; plan R1 is correct call but must not stop at generic SEARCH
SCHEMA bootstrap vs schema_migrations module: both run on State() open; CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent
recent_events flattened id shape vs read_events_since nested payload: intentional, P5 regression test planned
missing_evidence: Captured EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN diff with vs without idx_events_run_event in-repo
Concrete fixture spec (row counts / omit idx_events_run) that forces idx_events_run_event selection

critical_review.strongest_objection: Plan R1's no-SCAN + generic indexed SEARCH invariant is trivially satisfiable without idx_events_run_event: EXPLAIN on the tail query with only idx_events_run yields SEARCH idx_events_run plus TEMP B-TREE FOR ORDER BY, not a full scan—so P2 could greenwash while the new composite index is unused or absent from the query path.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that read_events_since already exists with conflicting semantics, that idx_events_run cannot satisfy the tail query at production scale (making the new index unnecessary), or that the plan expands beyond state.py/tests into telegram_progress or MCP transport would move this to revise/deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:

old databases upgraded on State() open via re-run SCHEMA
disconnect/reconnect consumer catches up exactly once via stored max cursor

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

payload.content:
Independently review the outcome_review gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Add the event-tail read cursor plus index that lets a consumer follow the event log incrementally, so an MCP transport drop can reconnect and catch up instead of restarting. The exact gap is that recent_events is a last-N read, there is no read_events_since WHERE event_id > ?, the events index is currently (run_id, ts) rather than (run_id, event_id), and run_watches.last_event_id has no generic after-this-id read behind it. Scope: add State.read_events_since(run_id, after_event_id, limit) using event_id > cursor ORDER BY event_id ASC LIMIT, add CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_run_event ON events(run_id, event_id) while keeping idx_events_run, prove the writer/reader boundary with a disconnect/reconnect consumer test, tolerate non-contiguous AUTOINCREMENT event ids, preserve recent_events/read_dual_agent_gate_events/replay behavior, and keep full suite green. Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Non-goals: do not make events the sole source of truth, do not change submit/job semantics, terminal-result storage, production Telegram progress, or resumable MCP transport.
